Parallel implementation alongside V1 on /api/v2/.
V1 remains untouched on /api/v1/.

Architecture:
- Domain/Application/Infrastructure/Presentation layers per module
- Constructor injection (no singleton imports)
- Typed AppErrors with HTTP status codes
- Zod validation in use cases
- AsyncLocalStorage request-ID correlation in logger

Modules implemented:
- auth: JWT + in-memory session store + one-time SSE tickets
- plants: N+1 query fixed (1 JOIN instead of 101 queries for 50 plants)
- watering: full CRUD with fertilizer types
- substrate: N+1 fixed, upsert components, owner checks
- components: admin-only CRUD, JOIN-based image fetch
- images: Multer + EXIF extraction + Sharp processing pipeline
- sales: 9 scrapers, SSE streaming, concurrency limiter
- more-info: OpenAI streaming + parallel plant link searchers

Database:
- database-v2.sql: full schema with utf8mb4
- 12 performance indexes (fixes full-table scans from V1)
- Consistent use of separate join tables (plant_images,
  substrate_images, component_images) — removes broken
  entity_images reference in MySQLPlantRepository

* feat: implement SSE ticket authentication and improve user data retrieval in auth middleware
refactor: update repository methods to simplify updates and enhance error handling

- Added tests for plant-related use cases covering creation, updates, deletions, and retrievals.
- Developed tests for sales entity and scraping helpers, ensuring correct parsing and URL handling.
- Established tests for MySQL repository implementations to verify SQL generation and data mapping.
- Configured Vitest for the backend test suite, including coverage reporting and path aliases.

* refactor(server): improve code formatting and dotenv configuration
refactor(images): update image upload middleware to ignore unused response parameter
refactor(exif-parser): change export style for create function

fix: chat stream content not displayed — remove context from content chunks

Content delta chunks included context.data_points, which made the
frontend's first if-branch (context update) catch them instead of the
else-if delta.content branch. The answer accumulator was never updated,
resulting in empty message.content and the question being silently
restored to the input.

Fix: send context only in the initial and final chunks. Content chunks
now contain only delta.content, matching the azure-search-openai-demo
NDJSON streaming protocol the frontend expects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ops): add APNS env vars to messaging service in docker-compose.prod.yml

The messaging service was missing APNS_ENABLED and related env vars in
its environment: block. It only received them via env_file (app.env),
but app.env doesn't set APNS_ENABLED — so the config default of false
was used, silently disabling push notifications.

Added same APNS environment block as notification service, with
APNS_ENABLED defaulting to true so it works without app.env changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: transition action UI + Playwright test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: duplicate Telegram responses from overlapping polls (#19)

* fix: add cron RPC method aliases for menu bar compatibility

The Swift menu bar client calls cron.create, cron.delete, cron.trigger,
cron.pause, cron.resume, cron.get, cron.logs — but the gateway only
registered cron.add, cron.remove, cron.run, cron.enable. This caused
'server error' when creating cron jobs from the menu bar.

Added all aliases so both naming conventions work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: include command field in cron.list response for menu bar

The Swift CronJob model requires a 'command' field but the gateway's
cron list adapter only returned {id, name, schedule, enabled}. The
JSONDecoder silently failed and returned an empty array.

Now includes command (from job.message), agentId, lastRun, nextRun.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: lenient cron job parsing in menu bar — handle null dates

Swift's JSONDecoder was silently failing because:
- lastRun/nextRun are null in JSON but Date? needs a custom strategy
- lastResult field missing entirely from gateway response

Added custom date decoder that handles null/missing dates, plus a
manual JSON fallback parser that constructs CronJob from raw dicts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: cron job detail view with run history in menu bar

Click any cron job → see its run history with status (✅/❌),
timestamp, duration, and output preview. 'Run Now' button triggers
the job and refreshes logs.

Fixes:
- Gateway cron.logs RPC now calls getRunLogs() correctly
- CronService adapter exposes getRunLogs method
- Swift RpcClient manually parses run logs (handles field name
  mismatches: startedAt→timestamp, status→result, etc.)
- CronSettingsView: job rows now tappable → detail view with
  back button, job info, and scrollable run history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: quick actions bar in menu bar panel

Adds a horizontal scrollable row of one-tap action buttons between
the header and chat messages:

- 🧠 Dream — run memory consolidation
- 🌅 Brief — morning briefing
- 📋 Status — show agents, memories, cron status
- 🧠 Memory — list all memories
- 🔄 Update — check for updates
- 📰 HN — top 5 Hacker News stories

Each button sets chatInput and calls sendMessage(), so the response
shows inline in the chat. Makes power features discoverable without
knowing CLI commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent 'tool_call_id did not have response' HTTP 400

Two fixes for the Copilot API 'assistant message with tool_calls
must be followed by tool messages' error:

1. Assistant.ts: wrap executeToolCall in try/catch — always push a
   tool response message even on error. Previously if a tool threw,
   the loop broke and subsequent tool_call_ids had no response.

2. messages.ts: sanitizeMessages now also strips orphaned tool_calls
   from assistant messages (where the tool response is missing).
   Handles both directions: orphan tool → remove, orphan
   tool_calls → strip from assistant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: UpdateAgent version detection — use readFileSync + skip -bar releases

- Import readFileSync properly instead of require('fs') (ESM compat)
- Fetch /releases?per_page=10 instead of /releases/latest to skip
  -bar releases (menu bar app tags were masking the framework version)
- Now correctly reports local:1.9.1, latest:1.9.1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* blog: What Broke When We Shipped Cron Jobs (And What We Learned)

Engineering blog post covering 6 real bugs from today's session:
- Silent cron executor (no logging)
- Swift JSONDecoder try? swallowing decode errors
- RPC method name mismatches (cron.create vs cron.add)
- Tool call loop breaking without sending all responses
- CheckedContinuation double-resume crash on wake
- UpdateAgent version 0.0.0 (require in ESM + /releases/latest)

Theme: every bug was a silent failure. Make failures loud.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram responses from overlapping polls

The polling timer fired every 1 second but getUpdates used a
10-second long-poll timeout. Multiple polls ran concurrently,
all with the same offset, so the same update was processed
multiple times — causing duplicate bot responses.

Added isPolling guard to skip poll if one is already in flight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: strip |||VOICE||| from Telegram replies + isolate cron conversations

Two fixes:
1. Telegram handler now strips the |||VOICE||| delimiter and only
   sends the formatted part. Previously the raw delimiter and voice
   text were sent as-is to Telegram.

2. Cron jobs using the Assistant agent now get isolated conversation
   keys (cron_{agentId}_{timestamp}) instead of sharing the default
   key. This prevents one failed tool_call from poisoning the
   conversation history and breaking all subsequent cron jobs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

SessionConfig.MCPServers, but shared copilot servers (connected
via dashboard.copilot_server) may not honor per-session MCP config,
especially if the server process is old. The embedded SDK mode
(no copilot_server configured) works reliably.

server is in use, directing users to remove dashboard.copilot_server
if MCP tools are missing.

commit.message:
fix: always queue fresh builds, never retry individual jobs

In-place build retries (PATCH ?retry=true) re-run individual jobs
which hit idempotency errors: artifact directories already existing,
partial state from previous runs, etc. The only reliable approach is
to queue a fresh top-level build.

RetryBuild now unconditionally calls queueFreshBuild. The artifact

Two learnings from PR review of ICM 756883304 hotfix:

1. Adding New Spec IDs Workflow: Always check the highest existing

   Run repo_validation after. Prevents ID conflicts and text mismatches.

2. Always Run Full Test Suite: When source changes affect a test
   project, run the entire suite, not just new/modified tests.
   Other tests may exercise different paths through the same code.

fix: use filesystem timestamps for actively running sessions

ListPersistedSessions was overriding the filesystem ModTime with
the DB's updated_at timestamp, which lags behind for sessions
actively running from the CLI. The DB only flushes updated_at at
turn boundaries, so a session mid-turn for an hour would appear
stale.

Now uses whichever timestamp is more recent (DB or filesystem),
and the hash function uses LastModified (which reflects filesystem
freshness) so the watcher detects activity from external CLI
sessions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial implementation of PR Conflict Detector

A GitHub Action that detects potential merge conflicts between open pull
requests by analyzing overlapping file and line changes across repositories.

Features:
- Hybrid conflict detection: file + line-level overlap heuristic with
  optional merge simulation verification
- Supports organization-wide, single repo, or multi-repo scanning
- Includes draft PRs by default (configurable)
- Outputs: Markdown report, JSON, GitHub issues, Slack notifications
- Dual auth: PAT and GitHub App with GHE support
- Scales to repos with hundreds of open PRs
- 138 tests, 100% coverage, pylint 10.0/10
